Why Neo Clouds, Electrification, and Hardware + GPUs Is a Structural Theme

What’s Happening on the Ground
• Nearly half of planned U.S. data center capacity for 2026 (~12 GW) is facing delays or cancellations
• Only ~5 GW of ~16 GW targeted capacity is currently under construction
• 30–50% of projects at risk due to transformer shortages, grid limits, and supply chain issues
• Tech giants like Amazon, Microsoft, and Google have committed over $650 billion toward AI infrastructure

Core Bottleneck = Power, Not Demand
• Demand for AI, cloud, and compute remains extremely strong
• The real constraint is electrification infrastructure, not funding
• Transformer shortages with lead times expanding from ~2 years to 5+ years
• Grid capacity limitations and delays in substation upgrades
• Supply chain dependency on specialized electrical equipment
• This creates a supply-side bottleneck rather than a demand slowdown

Why This Strengthens the Theme
• Demand is structural and driven by exponential AI growth
• Delays represent deferred demand, not destruction of demand
• Multi-year project pipeline ensures long visibility of capex
• Electrification ecosystem gains strong pricing power
• GPU and hardware demand continues to build backlog

Neo Cloud Opportunity
• Capacity constraints at hyperscalers open opportunities for new players
• Neo clouds focus on specialized AI workloads and faster deployment
• Smaller, modular data centers enable quicker execution
• Flexible power sourcing (renewables, captive power) improves scalability
• Constraint leads to decentralization of cloud infrastructure

Electrification as a Mega Trend
• AI data centers require massive and continuous power supply
• Each large data center consumes power comparable to a small city
• Significant investments required in grid upgrades, transformers, and switchgear
• Growth in energy storage and renewable integration
• Electrification becomes the backbone of AI infrastructure expansion

Hardware + GPUs Remain Core
• AI remains compute-intensive, ensuring sustained demand for GPUs
• Orders are placed years in advance, maintaining strong visibility
• Supply remains tight, supporting pricing power
• Hardware ecosystem continues to be a key beneficiary

Investment Takeaway
• This is a constraint-driven supercycle with strong demand and limited supply
• Leads to extended capex cycle and multi-year growth visibility
• Benefits extend beyond technology into power, industrials, and infrastructure sectors
• Creates a broad-based opportunity across electrification, hardware, and next-gen cloud ecosystems

🥇 INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS VS SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS: PORTER FIVE FORCES ANALYSIS

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Both companies operate in different industries with similar ROIC but significantly different competitive intensity moat strength and earnings quality  

INDUSTRY STRUCTURE

INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS  
- Operates in pre engineered buildings and infra segment  
- Highly competitive industry with multiple players  
- Price driven business with low differentiation  
- Impact: Negative — weak industry structure limits sustainable margins  

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS  
- Operates in niche bimetal and precision component segment  
- Limited competition with strong technology edge  
- Specialized applications in EV smart meters and industrial use  
- Impact: Positive — niche positioning supports strong margins  

ENTRY BARRIERS

INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS  
- Moderate entry barriers due to fabrication and engineering requirements  
- New players can enter with capital investment  
- Impact: Negative — risk of rising competition  

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS  
- High entry barriers due to proprietary processes and precision manufacturing  
- Long OEM approval cycles create strong entry barriers  
- Impact: Positive — protects market share and margins  

SUPPLIER POWER

INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS  
- High dependence on steel prices  
- Commodity volatility directly impacts margins  
- Impact: Negative — earnings sensitive to raw material cycle  

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS  
- Raw materials are metals but value addition is process driven  
- Better ability to manage input cost fluctuations  
- Impact: Positive — relatively stable margins  

BUYER POWER

INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS  
- Large clients with strong negotiation power  
- Low switching cost and tender based orders  
- Impact: Negative — limited pricing power  

SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS  
- OEM customers with long term relationships  
- High switching cost due to qualification process  
- Impact: Positive — better pricing power  

THREAT OF SUBSTITUTES

INTERARCH BUILDING SOLUTIONS  
- Alternatives like RCC construction and traditional EPC  
- Impact: Moderate — limits pricing flexibility  

 SHIVALIK BIMETAL CONTROLS  
- Limited substitutes for precision bimetal applications  
- Impact: Low — strong product relevance  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Interarch is cyclical capex driven and commodity sensitive  
- Shivalik is niche technology driven with structural growth tailwinds  

OUTLOOK  
- Interarch growth linked to infra cycle and steel prices  
- Shivalik growth driven by EV smart meters and global electrification  

IMPACT  
- Interarch Lower quality earnings with higher volatility  
- Shivalik Higher quality earnings supported by strong moat and stable growth

reports.text:
SAGARMALA PROGRAMME: KEY HIGHLIGHTS & OUTLOOK (PIB UPDATE)

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Sagarmala driving port-led development with large-scale infrastructure, logistics efficiency, and maritime capacity expansion  

SCALE & PROGRESS  
- Total Projects: 845 projects worth ₹6.06 lakh Cr  
- Completed: 315 projects worth ₹1.57 lakh Cr  
- Under Implementation: 210 projects  
- Pipeline: 320 projects  

INFRASTRUCTURE & CAPACITY  
- 7 coastal berth projects completed adding 9.84 MTPA capacity  
- Major ports handled record 915 MT cargo in FY26 (↑ 7.06% YoY)  
- Vessel turnaround time improved from 96 hrs (2014) → 49.5 hrs (2025)  

CONNECTIVITY & LOGISTICS  
- Strong push for multimodal connectivity (road, rail, coastal, inland waterways)  
- Inland waterways cargo increased ~7x (18 → 145 MTPA)  
- Ro-Pax & ferry projects improving logistics efficiency and reducing travel time  

INDUSTRIAL & ECONOMIC IMPACT  
- Port-led industrialization boosting manufacturing clusters  
- Sagarmala estimated to generate ~1 crore jobs (40 lakh direct + 60 lakh indirect)  
- Coastal community development via skill training & fisheries support  

COASTAL & SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT  
- 11 fishing harbour projects completed benefiting 30,000+ fishermen  
- 7,600+ candidates trained; 3,100+ placements in maritime sector  

SAGARMALA 2.0 (NEXT PHASE)  
- Budgetary Support: ₹85,482 Cr  
- Total Investment Potential: ₹3.6 lakh Cr  
- Focus Areas:
  • Port modernization & new ports  
  • Connectivity enhancement  
  • Coastal & inland waterways development  
  • Maritime services & R&D  
  • Coastal community development  

INSTITUTIONAL FRAMEWORK  
- NSAC: Apex policy & monitoring body  
- MSDC: Centre-state coordination  
- State Sagarmala Committees: Execution at state level  
- Sagarmala Finance Corporation (NBFC): Maritime financing (₹4,300 Cr sanctions)  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Structural transformation of India’s logistics ecosystem with lower costs, higher efficiency, and global competitiveness  

OUTLOOK  
- Long-term growth driven by trade expansion, infrastructure investment, and integrated logistics network  

IMPACT (MARKET & SECTORS)  
- Positive for Ports, Shipping, Logistics, Rail Infra, Industrial Corridors, and Coastal Economy-linked sectors

reports.text:
HINDALCO, VEDANTA, NALCO ;Global Aluminium Supply Hit; Prices May Spike to $4,000

• ⚡ Force Majeure: Emirates Global Aluminium halts some contracts after Iran strike

• 💥 Major Disruption: Al Taweelah smelter shutdown; damage may take up to 12 months to fix

• 🌍 Supply Shock: EGA contributes ~4% of global aluminium output

• 🚢 Trade Risk: Gulf region (~8% supply) impacted; Hormuz disruptions tighten exports

• 🏭 Wider Impact: Bahrain’s Alba & Qatar’s Qatalum also face shutdowns

DAILY MARKET UPDATE: KEY CORPORATE & MACRO DEVELOPMENTS

CORE HIGHLIGHT  
- Broad-based positive corporate developments led by capacity expansion, acquisitions, order wins, and restructuring; selective negatives from rating downgrade  

CORPORATE DEVELOPMENTS

RESTRUCTURING & BALANCE SHEET  
- McLeod Russel: NARCL-led debt restructuring approved; ₹1,050 Cr sustainable debt plan  
- ~10% equity dilution via conversion; discussions ongoing with remaining lenders  
- Impact: Positive — improves financial stability  

CAPACITY EXPANSION  
- TGV Sraac: Expanded chloromethanes & allied chemicals capacity (KOH, chlorine, methylene chloride)  
- Effective April 10, 2026  
- Impact: Positive — higher production & revenue visibility  

M&A / STRATEGIC MOVES  
- Mahindra & Mahindra: Exits Turkey foundry biz (non-core); improves capital allocation  
- Amber Enterprises: Acquires full control of Amber Resojet (subsidiary consolidation)  
- Larsen & Toubro: ₹1,123 Cr land acquisition via L&T Realty (real estate expansion)  
- Apollo Hospitals: Completes 100% stake in AHLL  
- Impact: Positive — sharper strategic focus & consolidation benefits  

ORDER WINS & BUSINESS GROWTH  
- Parth Electricals: ₹9.23 Cr order from Tata Power Odisha (first FY27 order)  
- Novus Loyalty: $407K international order (Bank of Abyssinia)  
- Digilogic Systems: Defence PSU order for radar testing system  
- Sonu Infratech: ₹1.03 Cr order from Reliance Industries  
- Impact: Positive — improves revenue pipeline & visibility  

PRODUCT / TECHNOLOGY UPDATES  
- AstraZeneca Pharma: Approval for expanded use of Calquence (CLL/SLL treatment)  
- Garware Hi-Tech Films: Launches smart films (PDLC, cloaking, graphic films)  
- Adani Green: Strong operational growth (capacity +35%, generation +34% YoY)  
- Impact: Positive — innovation-led growth & sector leadership  

OPERATIONS & EXPANSION  
- Isgec Heavy Engineering: Muzaffarnagar plant resumes operations  
- Kamdhenu Ventures: ₹13 Cr investment in paints subsidiary  
- TVS Srichakra: Global branding deal in US market (~₹8.5 Cr)  
- Impact: Positive — operational revival & expansion  

PARTNERSHIPS & BUSINESS MODEL  
- Soni Medicare: Partners with GMoney for patient financing solutions  
- Impact: Positive — improves affordability & service penetration  

FINANCIAL / FUNDING UPDATES  
- Imagicaaworld: ₹65 Cr funding arrangement (liquidity support)  
- Aditya Infotech: Credit rating upgraded to CRISIL A / Stable  
- TV Today Network: Rating downgraded to AA- / Negative  
- Impact: Mixed — selective stress vs improvement  

CORPORATE ACTIONS / GOVERNANCE  
- Swiggy: Leadership reshuffle; co-founder exits, new board appointments  
- iSERA Lifesciences: Rebranding from Covid Technologies  
- Vellora Impact: Name change approved  
- Impact: Neutral to mildly positive  

MACRO & GLOBAL CUES

INDIA MACRO  
- Forex reserves rise to $697.12 Bn (+$9.06 Bn WoW)  
- Strong external position supports currency stability  

GLOBAL MACRO  
- US CPI YoY at 3.3% (below expectations); core CPI stable  
- Signals potential room for rate cuts by Fed  

GEOPOLITICAL DEVELOPMENTS  
- Strait of Hormuz developments remain in focus (oil supply sensitivity)  
- US indicates contingency plans to ensure supply stability  

KEY INSIGHT  
- Strong domestic corporate momentum across sectors (infra, chemicals, auto, healthcare, renewables)  
- Macro tailwinds (FX reserves, easing inflation expectations) supportive for markets  

OUTLOOK  
- Markets likely to remain stock-specific with positive bias  
- Key monitorables: global inflation, Fed rate trajectory, geopolitical risks  

IMPACT  
- Overall Positive — strong flow of corporate triggers outweighs isolated negatives

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 10 April 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

POWER MECH: Wins ₹296 Cr Mumbai Metro O&M contract for monorail operations over 5 years.

UNIVASTU: JV wins ₹109.24 Cr government infra project for restoration works in Paithan.

MINI DIAMONDS: Bags ₹14 Cr domestic order for lab-grown polished diamonds from existing client.

PARTH ELECTRICALS: Secures ₹9.23 Cr order from Tata Power Odisha for 11KV substations.

NOVUS LOYALTY: Wins international order worth ~$0.4 Mn from Bank of Abyssinia for loyalty platform.

ALPHALOGIC: Bags ₹1.17 Cr order from Zepto for warehouse racking systems across multiple states.

SILICON RENTAL: Receives ₹1.17 Cr order for supply of MacBook Pro units to domestic client.

📍 ACQUISITION

AMBER ENTERPRISES: Completes ₹1,744.5 Cr acquisition of remaining 50% stake in Amber Rosejit.

APOLLO HOSPITALS: Acquires full ownership of AHLL with ₹18.69 Cr deal, making it a wholly owned subsidiary.

📍 COLLABORATION

VEEFIN SOLUTION: RR Kabel joins PSB Xchange platform for dealer financing ecosystem.

MOBILISE APP LAB: Partners KEENON for AI robotics distribution across India.

ECO HOTELS: Partners My Travel Bazaar to expand B2B travel distribution network.

SONI MEDICARE: Ties up with GMoney to offer credit/loan facilities for patients.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

PARK HOSPITALS: Expands network with Panchkula hospital launch and Mohali capacity addition.

📍 FUNDRAISING

IMAGICAAWORLD: Signs ₹65 Cr financing deal with Axis Bank to strengthen liquidity.

RBL BANK: Gets RBI nod to raise authorised capital to ₹1,800 Cr for Emirates NBD deal.

KAMDHENU VENTURES: Invests ₹13.01 Cr in subsidiary via rights issue for paint business expansion.

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

SHRIRAM FINANCE: CRISIL upgrades rating to AAA/Stable, citing strong capitalisation and MUFG backing.

ZODIAC CLOTHING: Assigned CRISIL BB/Stable rating for ₹53 Cr bank facilities.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

GODREJ PROPERTIES: Reports record FY26 bookings of ₹34,171 Cr with strong collections and cash flow growth.

GARMENT MANTRA: Reports ~60% YoY revenue growth in FY26 driven by exports and strong order book.

AJMERA REALTY: FY26 pre-sales at ₹1,701 Cr (+57% YoY) with record collections of ₹1,103 Cr.

NIYOGIN: Reports strong AUM growth (+26% YoY) and revenue growth (+56% YoY) in Q4.

M&M: Reports strong March sales at 1 lakh units led by UV segment growth.

ADANI GREEN ENERGY: FY26 capacity up 35% YoY to 19.3 GW with generation rising 34% YoY.

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

ISGEC HEAVY ENGINEERING: Receives approval to resume operations at Muzaffarnagar plant.

📍 CLARIFICATION

SUN PHARMA: Clarifies Organon acquisition news is speculative with no confirmed deal.

📍 LAUNCHES

HERANBA INDUSTRIES: Launches new crop nutrition products “Fentaamine” and “MycoHil”.

GARWARE HI-TECH FILMS: Launches advanced film products including PDLC smart films and cloaking films.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

KOPRAN: NCLT admits merger scheme with Kopran Labs, directs stakeholder meetings.

GENUS PRIME INFRA: Approves share allotment and capital restructuring under amalgamation scheme.

COVID TECHNOLOGIES: Renamed to iSERA Lifesciences Limited post regulatory approval.

PRATIKSHA CHEMICALS: Renamed to Vellora Impact Limited after ROC approval.

JINDAL POLY FILMS: Withdraws demerger scheme for non-woven fabrics business.

GENUS POWER INFRA: Updates cost allocation post demerger for tax purposes.

📍 IT NEWS

PACE DIGITEK: Achieves BESS milestone with 178 units delivered and 2.5 GW capacity operational.

📍 OTHERS

HIGHWAY INFRA: Withdraws NHAI LOA and faces 3-month debarment from bidding.

COAL INDIA: Absorbing cost surge to maintain coal prices, impacting margins.

RELIANCE / MRPL / CHENNAI PETRO: Refining margins capped at $15/bbl impacting profitability.

JP MORGAN: Cuts Nifty target to 27,000 citing global headwinds.

MANGALAM WORLDWIDE: Gets empanelled with QatarEnergy, BHEL, EIL & GSFC boosting growth visibility.

M&M: Exits Turkey foundry business, sells 90.4% stake in Erkunt Foundry.

Sources

Government defers annual shutdown of thermal capacity of about 10,000 MW due to gas shortages

Usually, during this period of the year, about 20,000 MW worth capacity goes under maintenance

Half of that has been deferred to meet the glut of roughly 8000 MW from gas-based plants

Tata Sons Listing Decision Likely Soon Amid RBI NBFC Status Review

Decision expected soon on listing of Tata Sons, holding company of Tata Group

Move seen as a way to bring stability amid governance concerns at Tata Trusts (66% stake holder)

Listing requirement linked to RBI’s classification of Tata Sons as an upper-layer NBFC

RBI had earlier mandated listing by Sept 2025 for such NBFCs

Tata Sons sought to avoid listing by surrendering CIC (Core Investment Company) status

Company has become net debt-free, supporting its case for deregistration

RBI has not made any company-specific comment yet

Inclusion of Tata Sons in NBFC-UL list remains under review (Jan 2025 list)

Revised framework for NBFC classification expected soon

Final outcome hinges on RBI decision on Tata Sons’ regulatory status

Addresses administrative delays before final decision stage

Expected to cut approval timelines by 6–12 months

Particularly relevant for biologics, formulations, and bulk drug imports

reports.text:
*Kotak Inst Eqt on TCS*
Buy, TP Rs 3100
Delivered an in-line quarter, aided by improved momentum in the international business and stable profitability.
Bookings were strong and included three mega deals.
TCS appears to be moving out of a patch of unusual underperformance, driven by BSNL deal ramp-down, insourcing and program completions.
Stock price has declined by 19% in past year, underperforming peers during a cyclically weak phase.
FY2027 should see a meaningful narrowing of the growth differential with peers.
GenAI readiness is also becoming more visible through targeted workforce interventions, broader AI investments and a sharper consulting push
Remain constructive & raise EPS by 3% on revised currency assumptions.

*CITI On TCS*
Sell, TP Rs 2250
Reported inline Q4 EBIT; FY26 revenues -2.4% yoy cc.
Forward looking indicators –
(a) TTM TCV +3.6% yoy;
(b) Headcount -4% yoy; (c) Management commentary – Getting into next year with positivity & confidence; they see a regular Q1 and Q2.
Expect low single-digit revenue growth to continue
Debate is likely to continue – TCS Q4/commentary had data points supporting both the bullish and bearish views

*GS on TCS*
Buy, TP Rs 2710
4QFY26 had no major surprises.
Key positives
(i) positive sequential revenue growth across all but one vertical;
(ii) Strong deal wins in 4Q, including 3 mega deals;
(iii) Modest headcount growth and earlier than expected wage hike;
(iv) company calling out growth in mid/large-sized accounts with early signs of stability;
(v) no discernible negative impact of AI on revenue growth, though re-investment to build capabilities is causing potential margin drag.
A few negatives
(i) No improvement in growth momentum, with +0.8% qoq organic growth for 3 quarters in a row;
(ii) Miss to guidance of better FY26 vs FY25 for int’l markets, with two consecutive years of close to 0% revenue growth and limited color on FY27 growth outlook;
(iii) Limited margin expansion despite strong Fx tailwinds.

*CLSA on TCS*
O-P, TP Rs 2985
4QFY26 was largely in line on revenues and Ebit margins and slightly better on order booking QoQ but not YoY given 4Q renewal seasonality.
Gen AI revenue at an annualised US$2.3bn, up 28% QoQ and 7.5% of overall revenue, implies AI should be a net tailwind in the medium to long term
On Middle East (ME) crisis, impact is limited to ME geography and travel vertical up to now, and c. 0.8% CC QoQ organic revenue growth in 4Q (like 2Q/3Q) does imply a still volatile macro at this juncture.
An attractive FCF yield of 6% (even higher than the Covid bottom) and a steep discount to Indian IT peers on valuations keeps us O-PF

*JPM on TCS*
OW, TP Rs 3150
Delivered a solid 4Q with a small revenue beat, stable margins, & strong deal wins, while commentary points to gradual improvement into FY27
International growth improved to 2% CC YY, demand is stabilising across Consumer, HiTech and Europe, with BFSI resilient and telecom showing early signs of recovery
Strong signings at $12bn and improving conversion support a better setup into FY27 as management sounds more confident.
AI revenues were up 28% QQ to 7.5% of revenues.
EBIT margins expanded ~10bps QoQ
Crucially, TCS continues to return 80–100% of FCF via dividends and buybacks, offering strong downside support
At 16.5x/15.5x FY27/28E, the stock trades ~2SD below its historical multiple and at a discount to peers despite superior cash returns and margin resilience.

*HSBC on TCS*
Hold, TP Rs 2755
Reported a decent quarter in context of widespread pessimism around the IT sector
Think stronger exit-rate & improvement in demand outlook positions it well for a relatively better FY27 over FY26
Expect mid to low-single-digit long-term revenue growth

*Nomura on TCS*
Buy, TP Rs 2930
4QY26 – broadly inline in P&L; three mega deals drive strong deal wins

*CLSA Equity Strategy - Vikas Jain*
After 18 months of being bearish, we turn constructive on Indian stocks
We may have surpassed the “maximum pain” point regarding both the Iran conflict and its impact on the market. With sentiment reaching extreme bearishness and valuations (on both an absolute and relative basis) trading below 10-year averages, we argue that risk-reward profile for Indian equities has finally balanced after 18 months of being unfavourable. Downside protection strategy helped our portfolio to outperform Nifty by 6ppt in last quarter. We now move toward capturing upside and make a slew of changes to our portfolio.
_*Portfolio Changes*_
We exit ITC in favour of Varun Beverages and from Bajaj Auto to Mahindra & Mahindra
We expect a better post-war set-up for Vedanta over Ultratech Cement.
We see L&T as a post-war recovery play and bring it in place of the defensive NTPC
We see better risk-reward in Bajaj Finance than IndusInd Bank
We raise O-WT on financials and cut IT to U-WT by swapping Tech Mahindra for HDFC Bank
We continue to hold ICICI Bank, SBI, ONGC, Tata Motors (CV + PV), Infosys, DMart, Eternal and Godrej Properties in our portfolio.

*Macquarie India Strategy*
India Has Underperformed Emerging Markets By 50% Since 2025 On Persistent Foreign Selling  
Still, The Building Blocks To Support A Near-Term Reversal Are Missing  
From A Negative View In '25, We Had Turned Mildly Optimistic For '26, But With A Disrupted Global Energy Market, We Revert To A Guarded Stance  
For Sustained Market Outperformance, India's Aggregate Earnings Profile Needs To Improve  
The Typical 500 Bps EPS Growth Premium To Emerging Markets Has To Resume

*Jefferies On Greed & Fear - Chris Wood*
India Will Also Be A Beneficiary, From A Relative-Return Standpoint, If AI Capex Does Peak This Year  
India Status As The Reverse AI Trade And Given That Foreigners Have Already Sold A Lot Of Stocks  
Still That Does Not Mean There Is No Downside Risk  
That Risk Is Not Only Renewed Conflict In Iran But Also A Sudden Cessation In Domestic Mutual Fund Inflows

Wipro

The Board will consider a proposal to buy back equity shares of the company, along with the announcement of results for the fourth quarter ended March 2026, on April 16.

HDFC Life Insurance Company

The Board will consider a proposal on April 16 for raising funds by issuing equity shares through a preferential issue, along with the announcement of audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 2026, and the recommendation of a final dividend, if any.

Tata Steel

The company has completed the acquisition of the remaining 10,000 equity shares (0.01 percent stake) in its subsidiary, Tata Steel Colors, from BlueScope Steel Asia Holdings Pty Ltd for Rs 0.03 crore. Post this transaction, the company's shareholding in Tata Steel Colors has increased from 99.99 percent to 100 percent, and accordingly, Tata Steel Colors has become an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of the company.

Poonawalla Fincorp

The company has launched its qualified institutional placement (QIP) issue on April 9. The floor price for the offer has been fixed at Rs 390.26 per share.

Saatvik Green Energy

The company's subsidiary, Saatvik Solar Industries, has received an order worth Rs 108.75 crore from independent EPC players for the supply of solar PV modules.

IFB Industries

The Board has approved the appointment of Manoj Kumar Vijay as an Additional Director in the category of Non-Executive Independent Director for a period of two years, effective April 9. It has also appointed Sandeep Joseph Abraham as Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer for a period of five years, effective from the same date.

IRB Infrastructure Developers

The company reported toll revenue of Rs 783.5 crore for March 2026, marking a growth of 20.7 percent over Rs 648.9 crore in the corresponding month of FY25.

Eicher Motors

Royal Enfield has launched its first electric motorcycle, the Flying Flea C6, priced at Rs 2.79 lakh. The FF.C6 will be available from April 10 at the first Flying Flea store in Jayanagar, Bengaluru, with deliveries set to begin at the end of May 2026.

SpiceJet

A UK court has ordered India's SpiceJet to pay about $8 million to an aircraft engine lessor over unpaid rent and maintenance charges for three engines, in the latest setback for the cash-strapped airline.
London's Commercial Court granted summary judgment on Wednesday in favour of Sunbird France 02 SAS over the unpaid rent and maintenance accruals, finding that SpiceJet had no viable defence.

The typical 500 bps EPS growth premium to Emerging Markets has to resume

**Fueling the Ceasefire Window**

India is moving fast to exploit a two-week ceasefire, pressing Tehran to expedite oil and gas cargoes to rebuild depleted fuel stocks. With sixteen India-flagged ships stranded and a massive backlog of 800 vessels in the region, the race is on to secure unhindered transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

However, "full normalcy" is months away. Broken energy infrastructure, limited insurance availability, and the constant risk of ships getting stranded again mean recovery will be a slow grind. To keep domestic prices stable, New Delhi is now banking on a critical US waiver extension for Russian oil, providing a necessary buffer while the Gulf stabilizes.

India battery demand is exploding for EVs and grid storage (to handle solar/wind) 

Government PLI scheme (₹18,100 crore) aimed for 50 GWh cell capacity by 2026 but as of late 2025/early 2026, only 1.4–few GWh commissioned. Imports still meet 90–100% of cell needs.

These gigafactories are annual production (GWh per year), not total batteries sitting in the grid. 

To store massive solar power, India must build factories and install batteries over many years. Even if all three hit full speed by 2028–29, it will take time to catch up.

The Indian ports are becoming the world's fastest ports now.

China coal to chemicals! AJC was the first one to put out that China clearly leveraged  the west-Asia turmoil to profit further from their coal based chemistry. Our report was out on 2nd April and now every big firm is sharing the same.

Japan's Daikin Industries is setting up its first R&D centre outside Japan in India . The ₹1,000 crore hub will be focused on chillers and AC systems for data centres, residential, and commercial use. The centre, likely near its existing Neemrana plant, will hire 500 engineers from top Indian institutions.

Beyond R&D, Daikin is also building a compressor plant, setting up a local refrigerant production entity, and investing ₹200 crore to locally manufacture data centre chillers

**PM KUSUM SCHEME: STRONG TAILWIND FOR SOLAR PUMP PLAYERS ⚡
**
**Key Beneficiaries:
• CAPTAIN POLYPLAST**: Emerging player building presence in solar pump + agri solutions ecosystem

• **SHAKTI PUMPS: **Market leader with strong execution and consistent wins in PM-KUSUM tenders

India’s push towards renewable agriculture is unlocking a massive opportunity through the PM-KUSUM scheme.

• Scheme to reduce diesel dependency and promote solar irrigation

• Subsidy structure makes adoption highly affordable:
– Central Govt: ~30%
– State Govt: ~30%
– Farmer contribution: ~10% (balance via loans)

• Execution model ensures smooth flow:
– Farmer applies → Nodal agency tender → Company installs → Payment released post verification

• Direct economic benefit to farmers:
– ₹50,000–₹60,000 annual savings (vs diesel pumps)

• Massive future opportunity:
– Target: 49 lakh installations by 2026
– Estimated market size: ~$29.5B

**BOFA: INDIA MACRO – BoP & CAD WATCH**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Balance of Payments (BoP) remains key concern amid widening CAD  

**MACRO VIEW**  
• Higher current account deficit (CAD) may make capital inflows challenging  
• External vulnerability remains in near-term  

**CAPITAL FLOW OUTLOOK**  
• Possibility of stronger capital inflows in H2 FY27  

**COMMODITY VIEW**  
• Commodity prices could correct sharply if supply-side risks ease  

**IMPACT**  
• Mixed – Near-term external pressure, but relief possible via flows & lower commodities

**MARGINS & PROFITABILITY**

• Margin compression in recruitment partly offset by improvement in other verticals

**SECTOR STRATEGY**

**KEY RISKS**

• Currency volatility  
• Commodity cost pressures  

**WHAT TO TRACK**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**

• Smaller cuts expected in FY28  

**SECTOR PERFORMANCE**  
• Nifty Auto down ~9% since start of West Asia conflict  
• Some stocks have corrected back to pre-GST levels  

**SECTOR STRATEGY**  
• Near-term pressure from input costs  
• Valuations becoming reasonable after correction  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Further rise in commodity prices  
• Demand slowdown  
• Margin compression  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Commodity price trends (steel, aluminum, etc.)  
• Margin movement in upcoming quarters  
• Demand recovery  
• Pricing actions by OEMs  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral to Negative (Near-term) – Cost pressures weigh on earnings despite easing disruptions

**JPMORGAN: POWER EQUIPMENT – STRONG UPCYCLE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• HV equipment makers in strong upcycle driven by renewables & grid expansion  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
• Multi-year growth visibility (3–4 years) backed by large RE capacity additions  
• India targeting ~470 GW solar & wind expansion  

**GROWTH DRIVERS**  
• Rising adoption of HVDC for renewable integration  
• ~80 GW evacuation projects planned by FY36  
• Implies ~$14–15 billion order opportunity  

**EXPORT OPPORTUNITY**  
• Global demand supporting export growth for Indian players  

**SECTOR VIEW**  
• Strong order inflows + execution visibility  
• Beneficiaries: Transformers, cables, EPC & grid infra players  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Execution delays  
• Supply chain constraints  
• Policy / regulatory changes  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Order inflows & tender pipeline  
• HVDC project announcements  
• Execution timelines  
• Export order growth  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Sustained upcycle with strong visibility across power equipment value chain

**GOLDMAN SACHS: MAX HEALTHCARE – QUICK VIEW**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Bhubaneswar entry offers strong growth with low competition  

**KEY INSIGHTS**  
• Under-served market with only ~4 hospitals in catchment  
• No major competition expected within 5 km radius  
• Supports faster occupancy & better pricing  

**STRATEGY**  
• Focus on selecting efficient, high-potential locations  

**RATING & TARGET PRICE**  
• Buy | TP ₹1300  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong expansion-led growth visibility

**HSBC: STEEL SECTOR – DOMESTIC PRICE STRENGTH**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Domestic steel prices expected to remain firm; downside risk limited  

**INVESTMENT VIEW**  
• Multiple price hikes already taken in March & April  
• Further hikes may be difficult, but current levels likely sustainable  
• Strong domestic factors supporting price stability  

**PRICING OUTLOOK**  
• Domestic HRC prices have seen consistent increases  
• No meaningful decline expected in near-term  
• Prices likely to hold near current elevated levels  

**EARNINGS OUTLOOK**  
• Upside risk to estimates due to stronger realizations  
• Higher spot prices supportive for profitability  

**STOCK VIEW**  
• Tata Steel – Maintain Buy | TP ₹250  
• SAIL – Maintain Hold | TP ₹150 (beneficiary of higher spot prices)  

**SECTOR STRATEGY**  
• Prefer companies benefiting from strong domestic pricing  
• Earnings upgrades likely if price strength sustains  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Global steel price correction  
• Demand slowdown  
• Raw material cost volatility  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Domestic steel price movement  
• China export trends  
• Raw material costs (iron ore, coking coal)  
• Infrastructure & construction demand  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Strong domestic pricing supports earnings outlook

**HSBC: INSURANCE & AMC – TARGET PRICE REVISIONS**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Broad target price cuts across insurance names; selective stability in AMCs  

**INVESTMENT VIEW**  
• Valuation reset across life insurers amid near-term growth moderation  
• AMC space relatively resilient with selective upgrades  

**RATING & TARGET PRICE CHANGES (INSURANCE)**  
• SBI Life – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹2300 (from ₹2410)  
• HDFC Life – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹700 (from ₹850)  
• ICICI Prudential Life – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹690 (from ₹790)  
• ICICI Lombard – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹2200 (from ₹2285)  
• PB Fintech – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹1980 (from ₹2190)  
• General Insurance Corp – Maintain Reduce | TP cut to ₹339 (from ₹345)  

**RATING & TARGET PRICE CHANGES (AMCs)**  
• ICICI Prudential AMC – Maintain Buy | TP ₹3600  
• HDFC AMC – Maintain Hold | TP ₹2580  
• Nippon AMC – Maintain Hold | TP raised to ₹860 (from ₹830)  

**SECTOR VIEW**  
• Insurance: Near-term pressure on growth & valuations  
• AMCs: More stable outlook with market-linked recovery potential  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Slower premium growth in life insurance  
• Market volatility impacting AUM for AMCs  
• Regulatory changes  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Premium growth trends  
• AUM movement & inflows  
• Margin trajectory  
• Competitive intensity  

**IMPACT**  
• Mixed – Insurance under pressure; AMCs relatively stable

**BOFA: CEMENT SECTOR – COST HEADWINDS & PRICING FOCUS**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Sector navigating rising cost pressures; pricing action remains key near-term catalyst  

**INVESTMENT VIEW**  
• Focus shifting beyond Q4 results amid upcoming headwinds  
• Ability to pass on cost inflation via price hikes will be critical  
• Cost pressures largely driven by geopolitical factors  

**COST OUTLOOK**  
• Estimated cost increase: ₹200–250/MT  
• Driven by West Asia conflict impacting fuel & logistics costs  

**RATING & TARGET PRICE CHANGES**  
• Ambuja Cement – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹590 (from ₹620)  
• UltraTech Cement – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹13,400 (from ₹14,000)  
• Dalmia Bharat – Maintain Buy | TP cut to ₹2,300 (from ₹2,500)  
• Shree Cement – Maintain Underperform | TP cut to ₹23,500 (from ₹24,600)  

**SECTOR STRATEGY**  
• Pricing discipline crucial to offset cost inflation  
• Margin trajectory dependent on successful price hikes  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Sustained high fuel & freight costs  
• Weak pricing environment  
• Demand slowdown in key regions  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Cement price hikes across regions  
• Input cost trends (fuel, logistics)  
• Demand recovery in infra & housing  
• Margin movement in upcoming quarters  

**IMPACT**  
• Neutral to Negative (Near-term) – Cost pressures persist; pricing action decisive

**CITI: OIL & GAS – SECTOR VIEW**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Shift to stock-specific approach as geopolitical risks may have peaked  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
• Focus on balancing medium-term fundamentals with recent drawdowns  
• Valuations attractive relative to historical levels  
• Recovery potential if “worst is over” scenario plays out  

**STOCK PICKS**  
• BPCL  
• GAIL  
• IGL  

**STRATEGY VIEW**  
• Prefer selective buying over broad sector exposure  
• Companies with strong fundamentals + beaten-down valuations likely to outperform  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Re-escalation in geopolitical tensions  
• Volatility in crude & gas prices  
• Policy interventions impacting margins  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Oil & gas price trends  
• Margin recovery across OMCs & gas companies  
• Government policy stance  
• Demand recovery  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (Selective) – Stock-specific opportunities emerging as risks ease

**MORGAN STANLEY: INDIA STRATEGY – RIDHAM DESAI**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Bull market on the horizon for Indian equities  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
• Trailing underperformance sets up strong recovery potential  
• Valuations, positioning and earnings supportive of upside  
• Domestic flows remain resilient despite sharp drawdowns  

**MACRO VIEW**  
• RBI actions improving sentiment on INR (still undervalued)  
• Strong policy momentum supporting growth outlook  
• Domestic bid continues to anchor markets  

**SECTOR STRATEGY**  
• Domestic Cyclicals preferred over Defensives & Export-oriented sectors  

**OVERWEIGHT**  
• Financials  
• Consumer Discretionary  
• Industrials  

**UNDERWEIGHT**  
• Energy  
• Materials  
• Utilities  
• Healthcare  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Global macro slowdown impacting sentiment  
• Delay in earnings recovery  
• Policy execution risks  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• INR movement & RBI stance  
• Domestic liquidity flows  
• Earnings growth trajectory  
• Sector rotation trends  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – Broad-based recovery expected led by domestic cyclicals

**BERNSTEIN: WEALTH MANAGERS – SECTOR VIEW**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Buy wealth managers for upside capture as market fear recedes  

**INVESTMENT THESIS**  
• Sharper drawdowns compared to AMCs → higher recovery potential  
• Operating leverage + market recovery can drive earnings rebound  
• Wealth platforms benefit from revival in equity flows  

**PREFERRED PICKS**  
• 360 One & Nuvama seen as most attractive opportunities  
• Anand Rathi showing surprising strength despite volatility  

**RATING & TARGET PRICE**  
• 360 One – Maintain Outperform | TP: ₹1360  
• Nuvama Wealth – Maintain Outperform | TP: ₹1710  
• Anand Rathi – Maintain Market Perform | TP: ₹3280  

**SECTOR VIEW**  
• Wealth managers better positioned vs AMCs in recovery phase  
• Strong correlation with market performance & client activity  

**KEY RISKS**  
• Market volatility impacting AUM growth  
• Slower-than-expected recovery in client inflows  
• Competitive intensity in wealth management space  

**WHAT TO TRACK**  
• Equity market direction & sentiment  
• Net inflows / client additions  
• AUM growth & margin trends  
• Performance vs AMC peers  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive – High beta play on market recovery; wealth managers likely to outperform AMCs

**JEFFERIES: INDIA ENERGY UPDATE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Ceasefire raises hopes of Hormuz reopening, but freight rates likely to remain elevated  

**KEY INSIGHTS**  
• GRMs at extreme levels due to ~3% global refining capacity disruption  
• Additional support from damage to Russian infrastructure  

**CRUDE DYNAMICS**  
• Persistent dislocations in crude markets  
• Russian Urals & Dubai crude trading at premium to Brent  

**DOWNSTREAM IMPACT (OMCs)**  
• OMC losses remain wide despite excise duty cuts  

**PETCHEM TAILWIND**  
• Massive petrochemical capacity damage globally  
• Supporting stronger spreads  

**GAS MARKET**  
• LNG prices up ~85%  
• Supply damage likely to keep gas markets tight in 2026–27  

**IMPACT**  
• Mixed – Upstream & petchem positive; OMCs and gas consumers under pressure

**India's Search for Balance**

India’s 2025 search trends reveal a nation deep in "digital upgrade" but desperate for offline connection. AI adoption skyrocketed by 154%, yet searches for "occupational burnout" and "micro-retirement" highlight a growing work-life crisis.

Culturally, the shift is fascinating: spirituality has gone high-tech with Mahabharat AI, while social lives are moving toward "aesthetic outings" and the viral bhajan clubbing (up 26.9K%). From Gen Alpha’s parental controls to senior citizens seeking tech for aging, India is searching for ways to use bots to boost productivity while reclaiming physical joy through "fake weddings" and hiking trails.

**The End of the IPO Honeymoon**

The era of effortless "listing gains" is fading. After the post-2020 frenzy, the IPO market is cooling as one-year average returns plummeted to just 3.03% in 2025—a sharp reality check for retail investors. In 2024, only 4 companies were trading above their issue price a year later, compared to 52 in 2021.

With ₹1.75 trillion in the pipeline, the market is shifting from "liquidity-driven" to "fundamental-led." Geopolitical tensions and tighter secondary markets mean fewer multi-baggers and more "wait-and-watch" caution. For the modern investor, the lesson is clear: the "easy money" phase is over; now, only quality and valuation discipline will survive the listing day.

**INDIA NUCLEAR PROGRAM: KALPAKKAM FBR MILESTONE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• India’s 500 MWe Fast Breeder Reactor (FBR) at Kalpakkam reaches critical milestone  

**WHAT IT MEANS**  
• Part of India’s 3-stage nuclear program (Uranium → Plutonium → Thorium)  
• FBRs can “breed” more fissile material than they consume  
• Key step toward long-term thorium-based energy strategy  

**REAL STRATEGIC VALUE**  
• Reduces dependence on imported uranium over time  
• Enables utilisation of India’s large thorium reserves  
• Strengthens long-term energy security  

**IMPORTANT REALITY CHECK**  
• Not “infinite energy” – still requires complex fuel cycle & infrastructure  
• Thorium cycle (U-233) is technologically challenging & not yet fully commercialised at scale  
• Multiple reactors & decades of deployment needed for meaningful share  

**GLOBAL CONTEXT**  
• Russia operates commercial FBRs; others remain limited/prototype stage  
• India among few nations pursuing full thorium roadmap  

**LONG-TERM OUTLOOK**  
• Potential to support a meaningful share of power mix over decades  
• Clean, baseload power with strategic independence benefits  

**IMPACT**  
• Positive (Long-term) – Major step in nuclear self-reliance, but gradual execution required

Domestic agrochemical performance remained muted due to a high base from last year’s inventory liquidation and seasonal delays, leading to flat volumes in FY26.

In contrast, exports showed strength, particularly in Brazil, North America, and Europe, supported by better demand and currency tailwinds.

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**

**KEY TAKEAWAYS**

• Strong CDMO demand supporting visibility  
• Improved operating leverage

**ADANI GREEN ENERGY: MACQUARIE UPDATE**

**CORE HIGHLIGHT**  
• Strong capacity expansion driving long-term growth visibility  

**RATING & TARGET**

**PINE LABS: JPMORGAN INITIATION**

**GROWTH OUTLOOK**

**TAKEAWAY**

• Neutral – Balanced risk-reward despite strong fundamentals

Morgan Stanley India Strategy

Ridham Desai

BROKERAGE RADAR

Expect revenue across coverage to grow in the high single digits, while profits are likely to rise only in the low single digits due to margin compression

India, however, continues to transition into a higher earnings growth phase, supported by sustained policy stimulus

Nine sectors are expected to register positive revenue growth, with Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services leading the pack

Utilities is set to report a de-growth in revenue

Growth in earnings is likely to be led by Communication Services, Materials and Consumer Discretionary

Energy and Gas Utilities are expected to report a decline in earnings

Bharti Airtel and Tata Steel should be the biggest contributors to aggregate BSE Sensex earnings

SBI is expected to be the poorest performer

45% of our coverage to see margin expansion

Margins are expected to rise for three out of 10 sectors

Utilities and Technology are likely to show expansion, whereas margins for Energy and Financials are likely to fall the most
